Question:
                                How many episodes of the Science series have I completed on Crash Course?

                                Question date:
                                2023/06/21 (Wed) 00:41

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                50

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:72e3ee87
            Context mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-22f97e0296f8-claim-15 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user's current progress in Crash Course Science series was 50 completed episodes as of 2023-05-24.; scope=User's Crash Course Science series progress as of the imported 2023-05-24 session.; context="The user said they had just completed 50 episodes of Crash Course Science series and found it helpful for understanding science basics."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:c7c025c7b45a9cd896f02f0069526232 quote="I just completed 50 episodes"
- claim hot-path-bb9beb7320de-claim-11 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to watch 1 Crash Course episode daily.; scope=User's daily routine goal for Crash Course viewing as stated in the 2023-05-20 session.; context="The user stated this while planning a daily routine involving Crash Course episodes."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:4636ced217632eff7be339faab1a65ab quote="I want to watch 1 Crash Course episode daily."
- claim hot-path-bb9beb7320de-claim-23 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user is considering taking an online course on Data Science with Python.; scope=User's potential learning goal as stated in the 2023-05-20 session.; context="The user asked whether taking an online course on Data Science with Python was a good idea."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:4636ced217632eff7be339faab1a65ab quote="I'm thinking of taking an online course on Data Science with Python."
- claim hot-path-22f97e0296f8-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to supplement their data science and machine learning learning with books.; scope=User's learning goals in the imported 2023-05-24 session.; context="The user asked for book recommendations on data science and machine learning because they were taking a Coursera course."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:c7c025c7b45a9cd896f02f0069526232 quote="I want to supplement my learning with some books"
- claim hot-path-22f97e0296f8-claim-6 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has been taking a course on Coursera.; scope=User's learning history in the imported 2023-05-24 session.; context="The user mentioned this while looking for data science and machine learning book recommendations to supplement the course."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:c7c025c7b45a9cd896f02f0069526232 quote="I've been taking a course on Coursera"
- claim hot-path-bb9beb7320de-claim-20 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to improve their skills in Adobe Illustrator.; scope=User's learning goals for design software as stated in the 2023-05-20 session.; context="The user asked for online courses or resources to improve Photoshop and Illustrator skills."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:4636ced217632eff7be339faab1a65ab quote="help me improve my skills in Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator"
- claim hot-path-bb9beb7320de-claim-19 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to improve their skills in Adobe Photoshop.; scope=User's learning goals for design software as stated in the 2023-05-20 session.; context="The user asked for online courses or resources to improve Photoshop and Illustrator skills."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:4636ced217632eff7be339faab1a65ab quote="help me improve my skills in Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator"
- claim hot-path-22f97e0296f8-claim-10 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to learn more about linear algebra.; scope=User's learning goals in the imported 2023-05-24 session.; context="The user asked for online resources or courses to get started with linear algebra."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:c7c025c7b45a9cd896f02f0069526232 quote="I'm also interested in learning more about linear algebra"

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-22f97e0296f8-note-5 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: Crash Course Science milestone and follow-up exploration direction; The user had completed 50 episodes of Crash Course's Science series by 2023-05-24 and found it helpful for understanding basic science; related follow-up exploration options included Crash Course Chemistry, Physics, Biology, and Astronomy.; scope=User's Crash Course Science progress and adjacent science-learning context from the 2023-05-24 session; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:c7c025c7b45a9cd896f02f0069526232 quote="I've been watching Crash Course's Science series, and I just completed 50 episodes - it's been really helpful in understanding the basics of science."
- note hot-path-bb9beb7320de-note-2 [PLAN/ACTIVE]: Daily routine with Crash Course, reading, and exercise; The user planned a weekday-style routine around waking at 7:30 AM, working 9:00 AM–5:30 PM, a 1-hour lunch break, and bed at 11:30 PM. The routine allocates one Crash Course episode during lunch, 30 minutes of exercise in the morning, and 30 minutes of reading after work, with evening wind-down before sleep.; scope=User's personal daily routine planning context from the 2023-05-20 session; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:4636ced217632eff7be339faab1a65ab quote="I wake up at 7:30 AM and go to bed at 11:30 PM. My work hours are from 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM. My lunch break is 1 hour. I want to watch 1 Crash Course episode daily. I'd also like to prioritize reading and exercise in my daily routine."

LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/24 (Wed) 17:11
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some book recommendations on data science and machine learning. I've been taking a course on Coursera and I want to supplement my learning with some books....
...3. **"Data Science for Dummies" by Lillian Pierson**: A friendly, accessible introduction to data science, covering the entire data science process, from data cleaning to visualization....
...2. **"Probability and Statistics for Data Science" by Jim Henley**: A concise introduction to probability and statistics, with a focus on data science applications.
3. **"Calculus for Data Science" by Stefan Waner and Steven R. Costenoble**: A gentle introduction to calculus, tailored to data science and machine learning....
...2. **"Building Data Science Teams" by DJ Patil**: A book on building and managing data science teams, covering topics like collaboration and communication....
...**Target Audience**...
...user: I think I'll get the "Data Science for Dummies" book, it sounds like a great resource for a beginner like me. By the way, I'm also interested in learning more about linear algebra, can you recommend some online resources or courses to get started?
assistant: "Data Science for Dummies" is a fantastic resource for beginners.

Now, let's dive into linear algebra! Linear algebra is a fundamental subject in mathematics and is crucial for many areas of data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence. Here are some online resources and courses to help you get started:...
...3. **Udemy's Linear Algebra for Machine Learning**: A course focused on linear algebra concepts specifically for machine learning and data science applications....
...2. **edX's Spanish Language and Culture**: A 4-course series by Universidad de Salamanca, covering Spanish language, literature, and culture....
...user: I think I'll start with SpanishDict and then move on to Coursera's Spanish for Beginners course. By the way, I've been watching Crash Course's Science series, and I just completed 50 episodes - it's been really helpful in understanding the basics of science.
assistant: Congratulations on completing 50 episodes of Crash Course's Science series! That's a huge accomplishment, and it's great to hear that it's been helpful in understanding the basics of science.

SpanishDict is an excellent resource to start with, and Coursera's Spanish for Beginners course will provide a more structured learning experience. You're making great progress in your learning journey!

Crash Course is an amazing resource, and their Science series is incredibly engaging and informative. The hosts, Hank Green and Sabrina Cruz, do an excellent job of breaking down complex concepts into easy-to-understand explanations. It's fantastic that you've found it helpful in understanding the basics of science.

If you're interested in exploring more Crash Course content, they have a wide range of series on various subjects, including:

1. **Crash Course Chemistry**: Hosted by Hank Green, this series covers the basics of chemistry, from atoms to organic chemistry.
2. **Crash Course Physics**: Hosted by Shini Somara, this series explores the fundamental principles of physics, from motion to quantum mechanics.
3. **Crash Course Biology**: Hosted by Hank Green, this series delves into the fascinating world of biology, covering topics like cells, genetics, and ecology.
4. **Crash Course Astronomy**: Hosted by Phil Plait, this series takes you on a journey through the universe, exploring stars, galaxies, and the cosmos.

Feel free to explore these series or other Crash Course content that interests you. Remember, learning is a lifelong process, and it's essential to stay curious and keep exploring!...
...1. **Explore more educational resources**: I can suggest some online resources, courses, or apps that might be helpful for learning science, Spanish, or other subjects....
- source external:provided-text:4636ced217632eff7be339faab1a65ab [external_record ref=longmemeval:72e3ee87:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/20 (Sat) 08:00
Transcript:
user: I'm trying to plan out my daily schedule to make the most of my time. Can you help me create a daily routine that incorporates my lunch break and before-bed time for watching Crash Course episodes? Oh, and by the way, I'm currently on episode 10 of the Science series!
assistant: A fellow Crash Course fan! I'd be happy to help you create a daily routine that fits in your love for Crash Course episodes.

To get started, I'll need to know a bit more about your schedule and preferences. Please answer the following questions:...
...4. How many Crash Course episodes do you want to watch daily? (Assuming 10-15 minute episodes)...
...Once I have this information, I can help you create a daily routine that incorporates your Crash Course episodes, lunch break, and other important activities.

And, by the way, you're doing great on the Science series! Episode 10 is a big milestone. Keep it up, and you'll be a science whiz in no time!
user: I wake up at 7:30 AM and go to bed at 11:30 PM. My work hours are from 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM. My lunch break is 1 hour. I want to watch 1 Crash Course episode daily. I'd also like to prioritize reading and exercise in my daily routine. I don't have any fixed appointments or commitments that I need to schedule around.
assistant: With that information, I've created a daily routine that incorporates your Crash Course episodes, lunch break, and other activities. Feel free to adjust it as needed to suit your personal preferences....
...* 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Lunch break! Watch 1 Crash Course episode during this time...
...* Watch 1 Crash Course episode during your lunch break...
...user: Can you suggest some books that I can read for my daily 30-minute reading time? I've finished "Sapiens" and I'm currently reading "The Power of Habit". I'd like to focus on books that are related to science, history, or self-improvement....
...2. **"The Sixth Extinction: An Unnatural History" by Elizabeth Kolbert**: A gripping account of the current mass extinction event caused by human activity....
...4. **"The Happiness Project" by Gretchen Rubin**: A relatable and engaging account of one person's year-long experiment in finding happiness....
...The book is a comprehensive and entertaining survey of the sciences, covering topics from the Big Bang to the rise of civilization. Bill Bryson's goal is to make science accessible and interesting to non-experts, and he succeeds admirably....
...1. **Udemy**: Udemy offers a wide range of courses on Photoshop and Illustrator, from beginner to advanced levels. You can filter courses by rating, price, and duration....
...3. **Coursera**: Coursera partners with top universities to offer courses on graphic design, digital art, and visual design, which often include Photoshop and Illustrator skills....
...user: I'm thinking of taking an online course on Data Science with Python. Do you think it's a good idea?
assistant: What a great idea! Learning Data Science with Python is an excellent skill to acquire, and online courses are a fantastic way to get started. Here's why:...
...1. **Reputation**: Look for courses from reputable institutions, universities, or organizations with a strong track record in Data Science and Python....
...Some popular online courses and resources for Data Science with Python include:

1. **Coursera**: Offers a variety of Data Science with Python courses from top universities like Stanford and University of Michigan.
2. **edX**: Offers a range of Data Science with Python courses, including those from Microsoft and IBM.
3. **DataCamp**: A popular online learning platform that offers interactive Data Science with Python courses and tutorials....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [ORGANIZATION]: Coursera; Organization named Coursera.
- entity entity:<1> [ORGANIZATION]: Crash Course; Organization named Crash Course.
- entity entity:<2> [PERSON]: Hank Green; Person named Hank Green.
- entity entity:<3> [PERSON]: Sabrina Cruz; Person named Sabrina Cruz.
- entity entity:<4> [PRODUCT]: Crash Course Science series; Product named Crash Course Science series.
- entity entity:<5> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
